_comment: The fail-closed decision table, shared by both SDKs.
The Python and Node suites load THIS FILE and iterate it, rather than each
hand-maintaining its own copy. The two SDKs make the same promise — LLM
traffic is protected or it does not flow — so the table that decides it is
one artifact. An earlier version claimed the suites mirrored each other
'case for case' and they already did not: Python had malformed-URL and
transport-failure cases Node lacked, Node had a default-URL case Python
lacked. Prose cannot hold two lists in sync; a shared table can.
Scope: this covers the ENV -> fallback decision only. Transport-level
behaviour (urllib vs fetch, what counts as 'down') is legitimately
language-specific and is tested separately in each suite.
Reading a case: given this env, does the SDK fall back to calling the
provider directly when the runtime agent is unreachable? false = refuse to
send (fail closed). PLATFORM_FALLBACK_DIRECT is explicit and always wins;
otherwise PLATFORM_ENV decides, and only a RECOGNISED non-production

cases.name: unset environment fails closed
cases.why: A prod deployment that forgets PLATFORM_ENV must not ship unprotected traffic behind a warning. Absence of information is not evidence of a dev box — the same rule the agent applies to AGENT_ENVIRONMENT.

cases.name: unrecognised environment fails closed
cases.why: A typo is the most likely way this is reached. 'porduction' must not resolve to the permissive branch.

cases.name: development falls back
cases.why: A laptop with no agent running must still work, or nobody adopts this.

cases.name: explicit false overrides a dev environment
explicit true overrides production
cases.why: An operator who explicitly opts out of protection gets what they asked for. Their call — but it must be explicit, never a default.

cases.name: explicit true overrides an unset environment
yes does not mean true
cases.why: The safe reading of an ambiguous value is the protected one. Only the literal 'true' disables protection.

cases.name: an empty explicit value does not mean true
